channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The return-to-work Survival Guide for new parents
item.description: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.content:encoded: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Virginia's data center boom is raising West Virginia's electricity bills
item.description: West Virginia is all-in on coal while neighboring Virginia is moving away from it. But the same utility serves both states, making it hard to lower bills for customers.
item.content:encoded: West Virginia is all-in on coal while neighboring Virginia is moving away from it. But the same utility serves both states, making it hard to lower bills for customers.
item.dc:creator: Roxy Todd

item.title: Nurse and PA associations sue the Trump administration over new student loan rules
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks American Academy of Physician Associates President Tom Pickard about a lawsuit seeking more generous caps on student loans for PA and nursing programs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks American Academy of Physician Associates President Tom Pickard about a lawsuit seeking more generous caps on student loans for PA and nursing programs.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: Police search for suspects in Ohio shooting that wounded 12 near a street festival
item.description: Police still had no suspects in custody Sunday after a weekend shooting near an Ohio street festival wounded 12 people and sent attendees scrambling for cover in a busy Toledo neighborhood.
item.content:encoded:
Police still had no suspects in custody Sunday after a weekend shooting near an Ohio street festival wounded 12 people and sent attendees scrambling for cover in a busy Toledo neighborhood.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. men's final tune-up games before the World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national team chose to play a pair of highly-ranked, super competitive teams in the final lead-up to the World Cup: Senegal and Germany. The matches showed the U.S. is ready.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. men's national team chose to play a pair of highly-ranked, super competitive teams in the final lead-up to the World Cup: Senegal and Germany. The matches showed the U.S. is ready.

(Image credit: Jamie Squire)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: SoFi Stadium workers vote to authorize strike ahead of World Cup
item.description: Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.
item.content:encoded:
Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Why one historian uses social media to remember D-Day in real time
item.description: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.content:encoded: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: From Ragtime to Schmigadoon: What to look for at the Tony Awards
item.description: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.content:encoded: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Despite a competitive market, finding a summer job is highly beneficial for teens
item.description: Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.
item.content:encoded:
Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.

(Image credit: Etienne Laurent)
item.dc:creator: Dianna Douglas

item.title: Visiting a beautiful oasis in Phoenix's Rio Salado Habitat Restoration Area
item.description: Tucked away in hot, dry, concrete and asphalt sprawl of Phoenix is a small sliver of the Salt River's green, damp natural beauty.
item.content:encoded: Tucked away in hot, dry, concrete and asphalt sprawl of Phoenix is a small sliver of the Salt River's green, damp natural beauty.
item.dc:creator: Alex Hager
